title: Apinagia glaziovii
taxonomy: Apinagia glaziovii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Podostemaceae. This family, also known as the "riverweed family," is an aquatic plant group that is native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. The plant is endemic to Brazil, where it grows in freshwater habitats. Apinagia glaziovii is further classified as a member of the genus Apinagia, which is a group of plants that are characterized by their unique, submerged aquatic habit. The classification of Apinagia glaziovii is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade rosids, order Malpighiales, family Podostemaceae, genus Apinagia, species A. glaziovii.
